summary: Adversarially design closure edge-case scenarios to stress-test a core VM closure-capture fix
logs: collected 38 closure scenarios across 6 risk areas

scenarios.name: five_level_chain_read_after_churn
scenarios.rationale: Captured var lives 5 frames up (a,b,c,d) after every intermediate frame returned AND 40 recursive churn calls reuse those exact slots; tests the full Parent chain, not just the immediate captured frame.

scenarios.name: deep_mutation_seen_by_sibling_after_churn
scenarios.rationale: Mutation via storeScopeChain writes into a grandparent captured map shared by two distinct closures (bump and reader); churn between every mutation must not wipe acc, and the sibling reader must observe the SAME map by reference.

scenarios.name: early_closure_survives_self_recursion
scenarios.rationale: build recurses into its OWN slot repeatedly; each level's 'peek' closure captures that level's distinct frame. If recursion reuse wipes an earlier level's captured map, combine() at level 3 (300) + level2(200)+level1(100)+level0(0)=600 breaks.

scenarios.name: map_callback_closures_capture_distinct_frames
scenarios.rationale: Four closures are created in successive .map callback invocations that reuse the same callback frame slot; each must keep its own 'local' AND share the grandparent 'base' map. churn afterward reuses the makeAdders frame slot too. 4*1000 + (10+20+30+40)=4100.

scenarios.name: mutual_capture_outer_outer_invoked_last
scenarios.rationale: leaf reaches up TWO levels for counter (top) and ONE for factor (mid); mutate writes counter two levels up via storeScopeChain. After two mutations interleaved with churn, counter=9, factor=3 -> leaf=27. Tests reference semantics across a 2-level Parent link with intervening slot churn.

scenarios.name: grandparent_alive_when_only_child_captured
scenarios.rationale: The deepest closure c captures p (parent frame) and g (grandparent gp frame), but only c's immediate frame is marked captured at creation; both gp and parent frames already returned. If the Parent link to gp's map is not kept alive independently, g (5) is lost and 16 breaks. 45 noise calls churn all intervening slots.

scenarios.name: factory_capture_then_deep_self_recursion
scenarios.rationale: Creates a captured closure (add100 over base=100), THEN runs 40 levels of self-recursion that cycle frame slots; if the recursion reuses add100's slot without honoring captured, base is wiped and v becomes nil+5 instead of 105 (105 + sumTo(40)=820 = 925).

scenarios.name: recursion_collects_per_level_closures
scenarios.rationale: Every recursion level creates a closure over its OWN local; all 6 captured maps must survive simultaneously while the same frame slots are pushed/popped. If a deeper level's reuse wipes a shallower captured sibling, the collected closures read wrong locals (expect 50+40+30+20+10+0=150).

scenarios.name: mutual_recursion_around_live_mutating_closure
scenarios.rationale: tick captures makeCounter's frame holding mutable count; then mutual even/odd recursion (12 alternating frames) churns slots; afterward tick() must mutate the SAME surviving map via storeScopeChain (1 then 2). Wipe -> nil+1; lost reference -> count resets each call (parity true=100, +1 +2 = 103).

scenarios.name: factorial_recursion_between_capture_and_use
scenarios.rationale: triple captures factor=3; classic factorial self-recursion (6 deep) runs entirely AFTER the capture but BEFORE triple is invoked, maximizing the window for a reused slot to clobber factor. fact(6)=720, *3 = 2160; a wiped factor would give nil*720.

scenarios.name: self_referential_recursion_via_captured_binding
scenarios.rationale: go recurses by referencing ITSELF through makeSummer's captured frame (scope-chain self-reference, not a top-level var) across 50 deep frames; getK was captured just before. Both makeSummer's per-call frame and getK's frame must keep distinct captured maps (sum 1..50=1275, +7 = 1282).

scenarios.name: interleaved_factories_split_by_recursion
scenarios.rationale: Three factory closures (tags 1,2,4) are created with 20-deep recursion runs interleaved BETWEEN each creation, so each capture survives multiple later frame-reuse storms. Any single wiped tag changes the encoded digit (100+20+4 = 124).

scenarios.name: recursive_factory_returns_closure_per_call
scenarios.rationale: makeChain both self-recurses AND captures n at each level via tagFn (marking every recursive frame captured); a captured frame must still be safely reused as a NORMAL recursion frame on the next deep call. Then makeFlag is captured and makeChain(4) runs again, re-churning slots (8..0=36, 4..0=10, +1000=1046... expect 36+10+1000=1046).

scenarios.name: counter_mutated_by_sibling_closure
scenarios.rationale: inc() reassigns the captured `let count` from inside a closure while a SIBLING closure get() must observe the same live map — proves both closures share one captured frame map (not snapshots) across repeated mutation.

scenarios.name: mutate_after_intervening_frame_reuse
scenarios.rationale: noise() runs between closure creation and each mutation, recycling the pooled frame slot that held the captured `let n`; if the captured flag fails to preserve the map, the reassignment lands on a wiped/fresh map and bump() returns wrong values (first should be 15, second 20).

scenarios.name: two_independent_counters_no_crosstalk
scenarios.rationale: Two counters created back-to-back reuse the same pooled frame slot in sequence; each must capture its OWN map. Interleaved mutation (c1,c2,c1,c2,c1) would corrupt if the second creation wiped the first's captured map or if reassignment leaked across maps.

scenarios.name: accumulator_mutated_inside_foreach_callback
scenarios.rationale: The forEach callback is a NESTED closure whose `total = total + x` must reach through TWO scope-chain links (callback -> add frame -> makeAcc captured map) via storeScopeChain. Callback frames are pushed/reused per element; the enclosing captured map must survive so peek() (a sibling) sees 6.

scenarios.name: reassign_captured_let_with_new_closure
scenarios.rationale: A captured `let op` (holding a closure) is REASSIGNED to a brand-new closure from inside setDouble(); run() must observe the NEW function by reference, not a snapshot of the original — stresses that storeScopeChain writes the latest value into the shared captured map that run() reads.

scenarios.name: deep_chain_mutation_with_reuse_pressure
scenarios.rationale: Multi-level chain: inner mutates `mult` (mid's captured map) AND `base` (outer's captured map) — two separate enclosing frames each marked captured at their own creation. Between calls, spin() (itself using a forEach callback closure) churns pooled frame slots. A missed captured flag on EITHER enclosing frame would wipe its map; readBase (a sibling of innerFn over outer) cross-checks base reached 3. Expect x1=13, x2=24, finalBase=3.

scenarios.name: fanout_distinct_captures_then_slot_churn
scenarios.rationale: One frame fans out 3 closures over distinct names sharing the single f.Vars map; after the factory returns, many noise() calls via .map reuse that depth-1 slot, then all 3 closures are invoked and must still see 10/20/30 (60), plus churn[0]=noise(1)=2+3+4=9, giving 69.

scenarios.name: fanout_two_batches_interleaved_same_slot
scenarios.rationale: makeBatch reuses the SAME depth-1 slot twice: first call captures map M1 (x=100, captured=true), noise churn must NOT wipe M1, then the second call must get a FRESH map M2 (x=200) without clobbering M1. batch1=101+102+103=306, batch2=201+202+203=606, total=912.

scenarios.name: fanout_closures_invoked_via_map_callback
scenarios.rationale: The fanned-out closures are invoked from inside a .map callback (itself running under ExecuteLambda, which pushes frames), so the invocation itself churns slots while two factory batches must keep distinct captures: f1 a=3,b=6 and f2 a=5,b=10 => 3+6+5+10=24.

scenarios.name: fanout_multilevel_scope_chain
scenarios.rationale: Fanned-out leaf closures read o from the OUTER frame (Parent chain) and i from the INNER frame; BOTH enclosing frames must be flagged captured. After outer/inner return, noise churn reuses both depth-1 and depth-2 slots. o=10,i=11: fns[0]=21, fns[1]=110, sum=131; churn[3]=noise(4)=11; 131+11=142.

scenarios.name: fanout_mutated_shared_counter_reference
scenarios.rationale: Two fanned-out closures (inc and get) SHARE the same captured n by reference; after the factory returns and noise churns the slot, three inc() mutations must persist in the surviving captured map and be visible to get(). get()=3, churn[2]=noise(3)=9, 3+9=12.

scenarios.name: fanout_array_of_distinct_via_helper_then_burn
scenarios.rationale: Each make(v) is a one-frame fan-out producing a closure over its own v, all created by reusing the SAME depth-1 frame slot across four .map iterations; then heavy noise churn reuses that exact slot repeatedly, after which all four closures must still return 11/22/33/44 => 110, proving captured=true forces a fresh map on every reuse instead of wiping the prior capture.

scenarios.name: closures_built_in_map_called_after
scenarios.rationale: The canonical loop-variable capture: each .map iteration reuses the SAME callback frame slot; iteration i+1 must see captured=true and allocate a fresh map, or all three closures collapse onto the last n (would give 90) or see a wiped nil (would give 0/NaN).

scenarios.name: map_closures_survive_later_unrelated_call
scenarios.rationale: After building closures inside .map, a deep recursive churn() aggressively reuses the pooled frame slots (including the one the map callback used). If the map-callback frames were not each freshly allocated on capture, churn's locals overwrite the captured n's and the sum breaks (expect 10).

scenarios.name: foreach_mutates_captured_accumulator
scenarios.rationale: Reference semantics under iteration: each forEach callback runs in a reused frame and must write total/count THROUGH the scope chain (storeScopeChain) to the SAME top-level map every iteration. If a fresh map were wrongly allocated mid-iteration, the accumulator would reset (expect total=21, count=3 -> 2103).

scenarios.name: nested_map_two_level_capture
scenarios.rationale: Two reused frame levels at once: the inner-map callback frame is reused across inner iterations AND across each outer iteration, while the leaf closure reads its own b (inner frame) plus a via Parent.Scope (outer-map callback frame). Both levels must stay captured independently or pairs cross-contaminate (correct: 11+21+12+22 = 66).

scenarios.name: filter_then_invoke_factory_closures
scenarios.rationale: Each .map closure has a TWO-link chain: its own n (reused map-callback frame) plus base from the enclosing makeAdders frame (Parent.Scope). The closures are then sieved by .filter (more callback-frame reuse) before being called. Surviving indices 0,2,3 give 101+103+104 = 311; a missed capture link would drop base or alias n.

scenarios.name: map_closure_outer_param_with_intervening_call
scenarios.rationale: Stress the Parent.Scope link specifically: closures capture n (map-callback frame) and offset (build's frame). After the map completes, build returns and unrelated noise() calls churn the same frame slots. If build's frame map were recycled (its captured flag not respected because the capture happened in a NESTED map-callback frame, not build's own frame), offset would vanish; correct sum 53+56 = 109.

scenarios.name: interleaved_counter_factory_churn
scenarios.rationale: Five mutating counter closures are created, then a deep recursive burn() churns the exact frame slots they captured, then they are mutated in interleaved (non-creation) order; if any captured 'count' map was wiped or aliased, the final per-counter sums (3+12+22+31+41) break.

scenarios.name: triple_nested_chain_out_of_order
scenarios.rationale: A closure-capturing-closure-capturing-closure: each leaf reads grandparent a, parent b, own c via the Parent scope chain. The intermediate level1/level2 frames are reused by deep() churn before the leaves run, so any missed 'captured' marking on a NON-defining enclosing frame wipes a or b; correct sums are 123, 456, 789.

scenarios.name: closures_invoke_each_other_reverse
scenarios.rationale: Composed closures hold other closures (compose's h captures f and g, which are themselves base-capturing closures); heavy spin() churn runs between build and invocation, and the two stacks are invoked in reverse order. stack1(0)=10101, stack2(7)=10117; any wiped 'base' or wiped f/g reference corrupts the total.

scenarios.name: captured_array_mutation_after_churn
scenarios.rationale: Each bag's push/sum closures share a captured 'items' array (reference semantics); recurse() churns frames AND drives forEach callbacks through ExecuteLambda (the inline-CALL path that never sets captured), then bags are mutated/read interleaved. bagX=1+2+4=7, bagY=10+20=30, bagZ=100+200=300, plus cross via forEach sum; correct total is 637.

scenarios.name: factory_array_then_map_churn
scenarios.rationale: Closures are created INSIDE a .map callback (ExecuteLambda path), so each makeGetter frame is captured while running under ExecuteLambda rather than Run(); churn() then creates+discards more getters reusing those slots, and the stored getters are invoked out of index order. Each must still see its own n: 16+1+25+9+4 = 55.

scenarios.name: shadowed_names_interleaved_churn
scenarios.rationale: All closures capture a variable literally named 'x', and loop()'s churn frames ALSO bind a local 'x' (plus a shadowing inner 'x'); if a stale reused map isn't fully replaced/cleared, a captured x could read a leftover churn 'x'. Out-of-order invocation must still yield 1004+1002+1001+1003 = 4010.

scenarios.name: deep_chain_partial_capture_reverse
scenarios.rationale: Four-level scope chain (leaf reads p,q,r,s across four distinct enclosing frames). grind() pushes four-deep recursion to maximize reuse of every chain level's slot before the leaves run in reverse order; missing the captured mark on ANY of the four enclosing frames wipes one term. L=10, M=100, N=1000, total 1110.
